parsed.fixed_xml: <ITEM>In dem Brandmüllerischen Buchladen wird ein <BIBL><TITLE>Project von <AUTHOR>Hiorini</AUTHOR> klugen und verständigen Hauß-Patter, wie auch avelichem Land-Leben</TITLE>, welches durch Sub. scriptionen in <VOLUME>2. Folianten</VOLUME> à <PRIZE>6. fl.</PRIZE> gedrucket wird</BIBL>. Ferner ist allda neü fertig worden: <BIBL><AUTHOR>Lutheri</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Bibel</TITLE> mit Figuren in <FORMAT>fol.</FORMAT></BIBL>; Item, des Herrn Abbts <BIBL><AUTHOR>Fleury</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Kirchen-Historien</TITLE> <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT></BIBL> aus dem frantzösischen ubersetzt; Item, <BIBL><TITLE>die gantze Pflicht des Menschen</TITLE> <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> aus dem Englischen ubersetzt</BIBL></ITEM>
parsed.explanation: 1) Removed the erroneous closing </TITLE> after 'Land-Leben' by moving TITLE scope to correctly include the whole title phrase with embedded AUTHOR. 2) Fixed malformed characters caused by bad encoding (e.g., Brandm√üllerischen -> Brandmüllerischen, verständigen, Hauß, à, neü, frantzösischen). 3) Kept and normalized the first bibliographic block as a well-formed <BIBL> with properly nested TITLE/AUTHOR/VOLUME/PRIZE tags. 4) Added a missing <BIBL> wrapper around the 'Fleury Kirchen-Historien' entry to make this item structurally consistent with surrounding bibliographic entries. 5) Preserved existing meaningful tags (<AUTHOR>, <TITLE>, <FORMAT>, <VOLUME>, <PRIZE>) and ensured proper nesting/closure across the whole ITEM. 6) Left historical spelling and punctuation largely intact (e.g., 'ubersetzt', 'Sub. scriptionen') except where corruption was clearly encoding-related.
